races.race_name: GAIN Equine Nutrition Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Alpha
runners.spotlight: Sea The Stars filly out of the brilliant Alpha Centauri; has an edge over stablemate Ibelieveicanfly on Leopardstown running when the pair were joint-favourites; Ryan Moore swaps places with Wayne Lordan; stable has won last four editions.
runners.comment: Superb pedigree, has an edge over stablemate Ibelieveicanfly on Leopardstown running
runners.owner: Flaxman Stables, Mrs Magnier, M Tabor, D Smith

runners.name: Isle Of Capri
runners.spotlight: First foal; by Wootton Bassett out of a 1m winner by Deep Impact; dam is a half-sister to three winners, out of 1,000 Guineas/Coronation Stakes winner Winter.
runners.comment: First foal by Wootton Bassett out of a 1m winner by Deep Impact, apparent stable selected
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg

runners.name: Alreem
runners.spotlight: E230,000 yearling; Starman colt is second foal out of a 1m4f turf/1m5f AW winner by Camelot; plenty of stamina in the pedigree.
runners.comment: E230,000 yearling, by Starman, winning dam by Camelot, plenty of stamina in the pedigree

runners.name: Ibelieveicanfly
runners.spotlight: Ryan Moore rode her into fourth at Leopardstown and now switches to runner-up Alpha; the hint is worth taking, even though Lordan was on the winner of this race last year.
runners.comment: Ryan Moore rode her into fourth at Leopardstown and now switches to runner-up Alpha
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Flaxman S

runners.name: Social Circle
runners.spotlight: First foal; Wootton Bassett filly out of a Listed winner by Gleneagles; dam is a half-sister to two winners; family of 2,000 Guineas winner Kameko; trainer has a senior rider on his other two runners.
runners.comment: Wootton Bassett filly out of a Listed winner by Gleneagles, yard's apparent third string

runners.name: Livenka
runners.spotlight: Dam has produced six winners, including high-class performers for this stable, Babouche (Group 1), Zarinsk (Group 2); looked in need of an extra furlong when keeping on for third behind the subsequent Queen Mary winner Victorious over 6f at Naas
runners.comment: Looked in need of an extra furlong when third behind the Queen Mary winner over 6f at Naas

runners.name: Exceptionally
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-sister to five winners, including the 2024 Derby hero City Of Troy; dam, by Galileo, won the Fillies' Mile and is a sister to Oaks winner Forever Together; apprentice aboard for debut, with Moore and Lordan on the yard's once-raced pair.
runners.comment: Half-sister to the 2024 Derby winner City Of Troy, apprentice aboard, best watched
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Samnina
runners.spotlight: E75,000 yearling; second foal; Sea The Moon filly whose dam was a Listed-placed winner in France.
runners.comment: E75,000 yearling, second foal, Sea The Moon filly, dam a Listed-placed winner in France

runners.name: Endless Night
runners.spotlight: St Mark's Basilica filly is noteworthy as a sister to the stable's Jersey Stakes winner Thesecretadversary; dam a Listed winner by Gleneagles, closely related to useful winners Wargrave and Ahandfulofsummers, out of a 6f Group 3 winner.
runners.comment: Of obvious interest as a sister to the stable's Jersey Stakes winner Thesecretadversary
runners.owner: Cayton Park Stud & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Old Joanie
runners.spotlight: E38,000 yearling; fourth foal; half-sister by State Of Rest to two winners; dam 1m4f AW winner, half-sister to 6f Group 2 winner Comanche Brave.
runners.comment: Half-sister to two winners, dam 1m4f AW winner and a half-sister to a 6f Group 2 winner

runners.name: Pretty Thoughts
runners.spotlight: E20,000 yearling; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners, notably Group 3 scorer Johan, out of an Italian 6f 2yo Listed winner.
runners.comment: E20,000 yearling, first foal, dam unraced half-sister to Group 3 scorer Johan

runners.name: Littlepacer
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and never involved on debut in the Leopardstown event in which Alpha was second; can be ruled out.
runners.comment: Slowly away and never involved on debut in the race in which Alpha was second, ruled out

runners.name: Exchange Student
runners.spotlight: Finished with only one behind her at Listowel; yard's newcomer Endless Night is more likely to feature.
runners.comment: Finished with only one behind her at Listowel, yard's newcomer is more likely to feature

races.race_name: Dubai Duty Free Rockingham Handicap (Premier Handicap)

runners.name: Red Evolution
runners.spotlight: Won twice last year over 5f at Cork and also finished second to Keke in this race, a fine effort for a horse of his age; not at best at Dundalk on only start this year; however, potentially interesting now that he is reunited with the champion jockey who rode him to a Cork win off just one pound lower last July.
runners.comment: Not at best at Dundalk on only start this year, fair chance on last year's strong 5f form

runners.name: Moltophino
runners.spotlight: Runner-up six times before belatedly opening his account in a premier handicap over 6f here last spring; may improve from his reappearance in the same race here in May;  a record of one win from 22 starts makes it hard to be overly confident.
runners.comment: A record of one win and six seconds from 22 starts makes it hard to be confident

runners.name: Buddy Batt
runners.spotlight: 3-8 at Dundalk, 1-10 on turf; fair fifth over C&D before unplaced behind Cuban Grey on AW; this looks too competitive for him.
runners.comment: 3-8 at Dundalk, 1-10 on turf, fair fifth over C&D before unplaced behind Cuban Grey on AW

runners.name: Back Down Under
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come at this track but over 6f/7f; good fourth over 6f here on latest; had an excuse on her previous start, she was reported coming in season; cheekpieces for the first time, may find a few of these rivals too quick.
runners.comment: Both wins have come at this track but over 6f/7f, good fourth over 6f here on latest

runners.name: Cuban Grey
runners.spotlight: Admirable sort with seven wins and 17 places to his name; faces a tough task in this grade; has struggled in two turf starts this month following a Dundalk win.
runners.comment: Admirable sort with seven wins and 17 places to his name, tough task in this grade

runners.name: Likedbymike
runners.spotlight: Nearly beat Stag Night in a C&D handicap before two fine runs at Listed level at Naas in races won by the subsequent Group 1 winner Mission Central, finishing closer on the first occasion; another fine effort when second in a Listed race at Cork; the downside is that she is 17lb higher than for her last handicap start.
runners.comment: Smart Listed form means she is 17lb higher than when second to Stag Night over C&D
runners.owner: Mrs Michael J Byrne & Jerry P Murphy

runners.name: Shadow Of The Moon
runners.spotlight: Two moderate displays at this venue represent a negative; fair second to Sarahmae at Down Royal last week; 2lb out of the handicap in this much more taxing company.
runners.comment: Two moderate displays at this venue represent a negative, second to Sarahmae at Down Royal

runners.name: Genesis
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner who got off the mark on turf in a 6f handicap here last month; third behind two of these rivals at Down Royal a fortnight ago; 3lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: 6f course winner, up in grade now after third behind two of these rivals at Down Royal

runners.name: Kerdos
runners.spotlight: High-class form for Clive Cox included a Group 2 win at Haydock in 2024; has failed to win since; last four runs for this stable not encouraging; mark sliding but needs to rediscover best; cheekpieces used for first time in Ireland.
runners.comment: Group 2 winner for Clive Cox, last four runs for this stable not encouraging, mark sliding
runners.owner: Michael Scaife/Liam Ferguson/Simon Murphy/H S Khunkhuna

runners.name: Dark Ace
runners.spotlight: AW double in Britain this year has taken his record since being gelded to 3-7; back on home territory after a satisfactory third at Newbury; now 15lb higher than for his last turf run in Ireland.
runners.comment: Has been campaigned effectively in Britain this year, two AW wins before Newbury third
runners.owner: J Fairhurst & Damian McCann & Cyril McMullan

runners.name: Erosandpsyche
runners.spotlight: Last win was in 2022; encouraging signs lately, notably a good effort in a recent Listed race at Cork, not far off Likedbymike then; retains enough ability to be competitive in good company.
runners.comment: Last win was in 2022, good effort in a recent Listed race at Cork, not far off Likedbymike

runners.name: Eclairage
runners.spotlight: Has only won on the AW but nearly put that right in a 5f handicap at Navan last month; not suited by the rain-hit ground when well beaten in the Dash at Epsom; failed to maintain a strong gallop in a Cork Listed race in which Likedbymike and Erosandpsyche were placed.
runners.comment: Four-time AW winner, has faced two stiff tasks since going close in a handicap at Navan

runners.name: Stag Night
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner; boasts a good record at this venue and is generally dependable; beat Likedbymike narrowly here on penultimate start; out of the money at Navan three weeks ago when Erosandpsyche was third.
runners.comment: Six-time winner, good record at this venue, beat Likedbymike here on penultimate start

runners.name: Gazelle d'Or
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate of 5-21 and better than ever in her last two races, finishing second from a good draw at Naas (5f Listed) before posting the same RPR over 6f at Fairyhouse.
runners.comment: Big chance on her Listed second behind Group 1 winner Mission Central, second again latest

runners.name: Toca Madera
runners.spotlight: Made all for an emphatic 5f Doncaster win early this month, his first success since August 2024; up 11lb for that; however, he had a higher mark early in his career when trained by Brian Meehan; definite chance, given the depth of the sprinting division in Britain, relative to Ireland.
runners.comment: Made all for emphatic Doncaster win, up 11lb for that, had a higher mark early in career
runners.owner: L Bond & Sheikh Aymen Jawad Al Hamandi

runners.name: Sarahmae
runners.spotlight: Specialist at this trip; has brought her total to five with two recent wins; up in grade and has to contend with a 7lb penalty for her victory over Shadow Of The Moon at Down Royal.
runners.comment: Specialist at this trip, has brought her total to five with two recent wins, up in grade
runners.owner: Mary B Hogan & Michael M O'Brien

runners.name: Harry's Hill
runners.spotlight: Very experienced sort who has recorded two of his nine wins at this track; up against it from 10lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Very experienced sort who has recorded two of his nine wins at this track. 10lb wrong here

races.race_name: Jebel Ali Racecourse & Stables Dash Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Oh Cecelia
runners.spotlight: In good form this season since joining this yard; started the campaign with a Naas handicap win off 72 (5f, soft) and now rated 101; beaten a head by Havana Anna in Naas Group 3 on penultimate; jockey reported she stopped quickly when last at Cork latest; capable of better but risky after that.
runners.comment: Progressive this season until below par run at Cork latest, better than that but risky

runners.name: Tahcawin
runners.spotlight: Fairyhouse nursery winner last season off 70 (6f, yielding); huge run in Naas Group 3 on latest when a close third, a neck behind Oh Cecilia (6f, good); enters calculations after that fine effort and place claims at least.
runners.comment: Enters calculations after excellent Group 3 run at Naas, place claims at least

runners.name: California Dreamer
runners.spotlight: 2-3 on the AW but remains a maiden on turf after 16 starts, although has run well plenty of times in defeat; not disgraced in stakes races at Leopardstown (7f, good) and here (1m, good) the last twice, but needs more dropped back in distance.
runners.comment: 2-3 on AW, 0-16 on turf, not disgraced in stakes' company last twice, needs more
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Big Gossey
runners.spotlight: Gallant grey veteran is an eight-time course winner and no signs of slowing down this season; won Listed race here in March (7f, sft-hvy) and another cracking run when runner up in the Group 2 Greenlands Stakes here in May (6f, good, Fregada 2.5l behind); goes on any ground, was fourth in this last year and another big run likely on the cards here.
runners.comment: Gallant veteran, as good as ever this year, fine Gr' 2 second latest, big run likely

runners.name: Tango Flare
runners.spotlight: Beat Big Gossey by 0.5l when winning the Bold Lad Handicap here last September but was in receipt of 10lbs; slow start to this season but back on song in Fairyhouse median auction race latest, winning it for the third time; unlucky in running when down the field in this last season; will like this ground.
runners.comment: Back to form in Fairyhouse race latest, unlucky in running in this last year, ground fine

runners.name: Silk Braid
runners.spotlight: C&D maiden win (yielding) sole start last season in October; excellent effort on seasonal return in Listed heat at Navan (6f, gd-yld), beaten 1.25l behind Charles Darwin; he has let the form down since, but this filly remains with potential and gets all the allowances here; not discounted.
runners.comment: C&D winner, fine Listed run on seasonal return, gets all allowances here, not discounted

runners.name: Bodhi Bear
runners.spotlight: Two handicap wins this season, over 5f here (sft-hvy) and over the same trip at Navan on latest (good) when scoring off 95; 6f no problem and finished just in front of Big Gossey in Listed race at Naas (5f, good) in April so not written off stepped back up in grade.
runners.comment: Two 5f handicap wins this season, in front of Big Gossey at Naas in April, not out of this

runners.name: Temperance
runners.spotlight: Won a Naas handicap off 75 last May; beaten just one rival home in three starts since and has been off the track since heavy Group 3 defeat at Leopardstown last July; impossible to fancy in this company.
runners.comment: Lost way since Naas handicap win, off since last July, impossible to fancy here

runners.name: Little Queenie
runners.spotlight: Likeable mare ran a cracking race on her seasonal return in a median auction race at Fairyhouse (6f, good) when just 0.5l behind the winner Tango Flare; rated just 92, however, so looks to be out of her depth in this grade (failed to trouble the judge in three previous runs in stakes company).
runners.comment: Cracking run on return at Fairyhouse latest, rated 92, out of her depth in this grade

runners.name: Fregada
runners.spotlight: Very promising start to her career, winning a C&D maiden in September 2024 before narrowly denied in Naas rated race on next start in June 2025; highly tried to no avail since but definite signs of life when not beaten that far in 5f Listed race at Cork last time (good) and wasn't disgraced in C&D Group 2 on penultimate; return to 6f a positive and could outrun likely odds.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner, signs of life over 5f at Cork last time, could outrun likely odds

races.race_name: Dubai Duty Free Derby Festival Handicap (Premier Handicap)

runners.name: Zarathos
runners.spotlight: Progressive British raider arrives in excellent form; notched up successive handicap wins at Windsor (1m, gd-fm) and York (7f, good) and another fine effort on latest at Epsom (7f, gd-sft) when a neck runner-up off 85; 5lb higher here so will need to improve again but that's not impossible; top rider booked.
runners.comment: British raider raised 5lb for latest Epsom second, top rider booked, must improve again
runners.owner: J Sarkar, D Sarkar, O Sarkar & D Sarkar

runners.name: Pretty Omagh Girl
runners.spotlight: Built on promise shown in maidens last year with all the way win on handicap debut and seasonal return over C&D (good) last month; handicapper took a dim view, hiking her up 10lb, and this is a much better race but she's unexposed so could be more to come.
runners.comment: C&D win on seasonal return/h'cap debut, up 10lb and this harder but may be more to come

runners.name: Far From Dandy
runners.spotlight: No win on turf since September 2024 but running well in defeat in good handicaps this season and rider's 3lb claim a plus; had Booyea behind when runner-up at Naas last time (1m, good); in the mix again.
runners.comment: Running well in defeat in good h'caps this term, rider takes off useful 3lb, in the mix

runners.name: Pierre Royal
runners.spotlight: Won a Gowran maiden (1m, good) for Dermot Weld last May (made all); ran better than the bare result on seasonal/stable at Limerick (1m, yielding) 18 days ago; eased 1lb for that and the return to quicker ground is a plus; player.
runners.comment: Ran better than result on stable/seasonal debut latest, eased 1lb, ground fine, player

runners.name: Playin Cool
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden at Leopardstown last October (1m1f, heavy); beaten a nose in a 7f (soft) handicap back there on latest; raised 4lb, this is tougher and has to prove effectiveness on faster ground.
runners.comment: Heavy ground maiden winner, beaten a nose on soft latest, quicker ground a query

runners.name: Coeur d'Or
runners.spotlight: Veteran's last win came over C&D in the Irish Cambridgeshire in August 2023; didn't get the clearest of runs when a 2.75l fifth in this last year and 11lb lower now; not beaten far the last twice, fast ground is fine and he's an interesting runner.
runners.comment: 2.75l fifth in this last year, 11lb lower now, not beaten far the last twice, interesting
runners.owner: Mark E Phelan & S P O'Connor

runners.name: Lord Massusus
runners.spotlight: Ground versatile; seven-time winner (including at Group 3 and Listed level); running respectably in stakes company this term and is 2-2 in handicaps, though hasn't run in one since winning off 1lb higher at Naas in 2023; course winner interesting with rider taking off 5lb.
runners.comment: Useful stakes performer, first handicap run since 2023, rider takes off 5lb, interesting

runners.name: Red Charlie
runners.spotlight: Won over 7f here (sft-hvy) on return from long layoff in March; raised 15lb and beaten a nose in follow up bid here (7f, soft) in April; beaten 4.75l into fourth upped to 1m at Naas on latest (1m, good) when 1l behind Far From Dandy; 2lb wrong and may need it softer than this.
runners.comment: Two fine runs over 7f here on testing ground after layoff, 2lb wrong here, ground a query

runners.name: Booyea
runners.spotlight: Won this in 2024 off 4lb higher, his last win; well beaten in repeat bid last season but then chased home Slieve Binnian in hot handicap at Leopardstown; not a bad run on seasonal return at Naas and soft wouldn't have suited on latest; could go well back on faster ground if the new blinkers provide a spark.
runners.comment: 2024 winner off 4lb higher, soft not ideal latest, blinkers go on, could go well

runners.name: Perry Mason
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner on good and heavy ground at 1m-1m2f; raised 6lb for May's 1m2f (good) win here and probably didn't stay 1m6f here on latest; drop back in trip a plus but may need easier ground at this distance.
runners.comment: C&D winner, raised 6lb for 1m2f win here in May, trip too far latest, may need it softer
runners.owner: Maurice Andrew Ahern & K Jardine & Alan Green

runners.name: Slieve Binnian
runners.spotlight: Won this last year off 83 and followed up at Leopardstown (1m1f, gd-fm) off 88; no joy in Bahrain in the winter; soft ground not ideal on seasonal reappearance at Leopardstown and is on last winning mark now, so can't discount.
runners.comment: Won this last year off 5lb higher, soft not ideal latest, on last winning mark, chance

runners.name: Orandi
runners.spotlight: Won the Irish Lincolnshire over C&D in 2025 but no joy since; midfield at Limerick on latest (1m, yielding) and remains 2lb above last winning mark; best form on easier ground than this; others appeal more.
runners.comment: No joy since C&D win in 2025, remains 2lb above that success, best form on easy ground

runners.name: State Actor
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner (on good and heavy) in 2024 but no joy since; beaten 1l when third in this last year and 1lb lower now but had the benefit of a recent run that day; might need this after 238 days off but Keane booked so market check advised.
runners.comment: No win since 2024, beaten 1l in this last year off 1lb higher, Keane up, check market

races.race_name: Dubai Duty Free International Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Purview
runners.spotlight: This progressive Kingman colt is on a hat-trick after successive Listed wins at Cork (10.5f, yielding) on final run of last term and at this venue (1m4f, good) on seasonal return; won that latest race by 6l in seriously impressive fashion (eased down) and should be fine dropped back in trip; top rated here and leading claims.
runners.comment: Progressive colt, seriously impressive 1m4f win here latest, 1m2f fine, leading claims

runners.name: Crown Of Oaks
runners.spotlight: British raider; three handicap wins last season at 1m-1m2f on good and soft ground, including the Balmoral at Ascot (1m, good) on his final start of 2025 off 99; ran well when third in C&D handicap last September; a bit to find on the figures here and lacks a recent run but respected.
runners.comment: Three-time handicap winner, lacks a recent run and a bit to find on the figures

runners.name: Hotazhell
runners.spotlight: Group 1 winner as a 2yo; came up dry in 3yo season but plenty of good runs in defeat, including when placing in the 2000 Guineas here (1m, good); has a tendency to race keenly at times, as he did when beaten a nose dropped to Listed company on seasonal return at Navan (1m1f, good), splitting Isaac Newton and Geryon; cheekpieces look a good addition and he should go well again.
runners.comment: 2yo Group 1 winner, no joy last year, beaten a nose on return at Navan, cheekpieces on

runners.name: Isaac Newton
runners.spotlight: Goodwood maiden winner on debut last August (7f, gd-sft); creditable run in Group 1 at Saint-Cloud (1m2f, very soft) in October; moderate efforts first two runs this term, but back on song when in front of Hotazhell and Geryon in Listed contest at Navan three weeks ago; needs to improve further to beat Purview, but solid place claims if backing up that last run.
runners.comment: Back on song in Navan Listed heat latest, place chance but more needed to win

runners.name: Geryon
runners.spotlight: Smart form here as a 2yo included two seconds in Group 2 company at 7f-1m on good and yielding to soft ground; back to form and probably unlucky not to win over 1m1f (good) in Listed heat at Navan on latest when a neck behind Isaac Newton and a nose behind Hotazhell (finished third/promoted to second); place shout with a repeat of that.
runners.comment: Back to form when unlucky not to win at Navan in Listed heat, place shout if repeats

runners.name: Sindria
runners.spotlight: Started season with back-to-back wins at Gowran, a 1m handicap (soft) and a 9.5f Listed race (10.5f, gd-yld); not disgraced in 1m Group 2 here (good) last month and again ran well when beaten 1l in 1m (good) Listed race at Navan three weeks ago; this longer trip fine and preferred by Browne-McMonagle, but rated 100 so needs to find more in this company.
runners.comment: Listed winner at Gowran in May, another good Listed run at Navan latest, more needed

runners.name: Trustyourinstinct
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner on the Flat; landed Gowran conditions race (9.5f, yielding) for the third year running on latest; won lesser renewal of this race last season (good) but was no match for Purview in 1m4f Listed heat here on penultimate; hard to see him turning that around, but generally reliable and place chance.
runners.comment: Won lesser renewal of this last year, no match for Purview on penultimate, place shout

races.race_name: Dubai Duty Free Celebration Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Alcantor
runners.spotlight: Ex-French performer won three Group 3s for Andre Fabre, all over 1m on rain-softened ground; not disgraced on debut for this stable on debut at Leopardstown (1m, good) but disappointed dropped back to 7f in Epsom Group 3 latest on good to soft ground; obvious chance on ratings here and preferred by McMonagle, but would surely prefer softer ground.
runners.comment: Three-time Group 3 winner in France, behind Tokenomics latest, best form on soft ground

runners.name: Tokenomics
runners.spotlight: Progressive gelding has taken form to new level this term with excellent Listed efforts at Leopardstown on last two starts; had Alcantor 2l behind when a 1.25l runner-up on latest (1m, good) and should uphold that form; has winning course form in the book and leading claims.
runners.comment: Excellent efforts in Listed company last twice, beat Alcantor by 2l latest, big chance

runners.name: Accredited
runners.spotlight: Won a Naas maiden on seasonal return in March (7f, soft); soundly beaten upped to 1m in Naas rated race on latest; Moore back on board and blinkers go on, but rated just 93 so looks to be facing a tough task here.
runners.comment: 7f Naas maiden winner in March, beaten in rated race there latest, rated 93, tough task
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: Zodiac Bear
runners.spotlight: Three of four runs have come at Leopardstown; won maiden there last May (7f, good) and fine 2l third in Group 3 (7f, good) the following month; not seen again until respectable 3l fourth in Group 3 Ballycorus Stakes there (7f, soft) 17 days ago; sharper for that and worth a go at 1m on that evidence; could go well.
runners.comment: Solid runs in 7f Leopardstown Gr 3s last twice, step up to 1m should suit, could go well

runners.name: Duckadilly
runners.spotlight: Gained first win at stakes level when taking a Listed fillies' heat at Naas three weeks ago (1m, good); seemed to appreciate the step up to 1m that day, but will need to improve again to make her presence felt in this deeper race.
runners.comment: Listed winner at Naas on latest, more needed to make presence felt in this deeper race

runners.name: Cowardofthecounty
runners.spotlight: Group 3 winner as a 2yo; lost way last season but back to form on last two starts; runner-up to City Of Memphis in conditions race at Gowran (7f, soft) and won conditions race at Fairyhouse latest (7f, good); much more needed up in class and trip and looks yard's second-string on rider arrangements.
runners.comment: Back to form in conditions races last twice, much more needed upped in class

runners.name: Spicy Margarita
runners.spotlight: AW winner is 0-11 on turf and rated 93; tailed off on heavy ground here on latest; better than that and was a decent fourth in a C&D Group 3 here last April, but hard to make a solid case for her on recent form.
runners.comment: 0-11 on turf and rated 93, tailed off on heavy here latest, hard to make a case for
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

races.race_name: Dubai Duty Free Irish Derby (Group 1) (No Geldings)

runners.name: Christmas Day
runners.spotlight: Maiden success was on heavy going and Group 3 Leopardstown win last October was on good-to-yielding; same going description when landing Group 3 Ballysax at Leopardstown, with James J Braddock back in fifth; outpaced on better ground when third in the Dante, 1 3/4l behind runner-up Action; rose to the occasion when prominently ridden in the Derby, with soft ground evidently in his favour; different conditions now, but his established stamina is a significant positive.
runners.comment: Rose to the occasion in the Derby when soft ground suited, different going, sure to stay

runners.name: Shaihaan
runners.spotlight: Improved from his debut when chasing home Pierre Bonnard over 1m at Dundalk last August; found the competition too hot in the Beresford Stakes; made a fine start to the season when winning a good 1m2f maiden at this venue; got upset in the stalls when failing to build on that in the Derby Trial at Leopardstown; better when third behind the subsequent King Edward VII Stakes winner Causeway in the 1m2f Gallinule Stakes here; first Group 1 attempt now, should stay 1m4f.
runners.comment: Third behind the talented Causeway in the 1m2f Gallinule Stakes, first Group 1 attempt

runners.name: Benvenuto Cellini
runners.spotlight: Killarney maiden winner last July; impressed in a five-runner Group 2 at Leopardstown (1m) in September; only third behind stablemates Hawk Mountain and Action on heavy ground in the Group 1 Futurity at Doncaster (1m) in October; stylishly won the Chester Vase, and led the four-strong Ballydoyle team at Epsom; never featured from off the pace after a controversial stalls incident; soft ground all against him then, and retains status as the selected of an unchanged Ballydoyle squad.
runners.comment: Impressive Chester Vase winner, mishap at the start at Derby, may restore reputation now

runners.name: Bunyola Bay
runners.spotlight: Finished third of eight, beaten 3l, when the well-backed favourite for a soft-ground maiden at Newbury last October (winner finished fourth in the Greenham); made all for 7f Doncaster win on seasonal debut; confirmed reputation as a progressive colt when landing a valuable conditions event over an extended 1m1f at Gowran Park early this month; dam has a decent record at stud, but has tended to produce milers rather than middle-distances types; lacks Pattern race experience.
runners.comment: Won a valuable conditions event at Gowran Park, not sure to stay, lacks Pattern experience

runners.name: Action
runners.spotlight: Closely related to Derby/Irish Derby winner Lambourn; Galway maiden winner; graduated to Pattern company with two solid displays, third to the excellent Bow Echo in the Royal Lodge, in front of Benvenuto Cellini when second in the Futurity at Doncaster (1m, heavy); tame effort when fourth behind Raaheeb on reappearance at Sandown; better when a front-running second in the Dante; dropped away tamely after making the running in the Derby; cheekpieces are left off now.
runners.comment: Fourth behind Raaheeb at Sandown, Dante runner-up, dropped away tamely in the Derby
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Pierre Bonnard
runners.spotlight: Asserted late on to finish firmly on top last October in two 1m2f Pattern events, the Group 3 Zetland Stakes at Newmarket (good to firm) and Group 1 Criterium de Saint-Cloud (very soft); 3yo campaign got off to a low-key start behind Christmas Day in the Ballysax at Leopardstown; much more like it when second to James J Braddock in the Derby Trial at the same venue; jury is out following a short-lived Derby challenge.
runners.comment: Big force at two, beaten by James J Braddock at Leopardstown, short-lived Derby challenge

runners.name: James J Braddock
runners.spotlight: Second start at two yielded a 6l course 1m win on heavy; never able to challenge on return in the Group 3 Ballysax won by Christmas Day; stepped up on that when staying on strongly to beat Pierre Bonnard in the Derby Trial over the same course and trip; coltish in the preliminaries at Epsom; did well in the circumstances to finish third (second-best of the Irish contingent); seems ground-versatile and likely to appreciate this stiff test.
runners.comment: Coltish in the preliminaries at Epsom, did well in the circumstances, solid place claims
runners.owner: Aziz Kheir Aust Partners & Kevin Blake

runners.name: Raaheeb
runners.spotlight: Sea The Stars colt has strong pedigree credentials as a brother to Baaeed and Hukum; showed strong promise in justifying favouritism at Ascot (7f, soft) on only juvenile outing; made an excellent start to the season when landing the Classic Trial at Sandown, with the disappointing odds-on favourite Action well held in fourth; fascinating as the relative unknown quantity here.
runners.comment: Classic Trial winner at Sandown, fascinating as the relative unknown quantity, should stay

races.race_name: Tulfarris Hotel And Golf Resort Maddenstown Handicap (Premier Handicap)

runners.name: King Of Earth
runners.spotlight: Promise on turf before taking advantage of a good opportunity on AW at Wolverhampton; mark left unchanged; cheekpieces added, merits respect.
runners.comment: Promise on turf before taking advantage of a good opportunity on AW, mark left unchanged

runners.name: Anushka
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Dundalk fillies' maiden last October; failed to make an impact when highly tried on reappearance in a Group 3 race won by True Love; closely matched with Sanctijude on running over 7f here in her first handicap; 1 3/4l behind runner-up Punica Granatum when third over 1m here five weeks ago.
runners.comment: 1 3/4l behind Punica Granatum here five weeks ago, that rival was making seasonal debut

runners.name: Summer Is Tomorrow
runners.spotlight: Looked well suited by 7f when winning twice at two; has failed to prosper in conditions races this term; others preferred.
runners.comment: Useful form at two, has failed to prosper in conditions races this term, others preferred

runners.name: Punica Granatum
runners.spotlight: Showed winning potential in making the frame in 7f maidens here and at Gowran on her first two starts; faced a tough task in the Goffs Million; again shaped like a probable future winner when a short head second to Green Carrera here on seasonal debut; that form has been strongly boosted by the winner in taking the Sandringham Stakes.
runners.comment: Went very close on seasonal debut, form given a huge boost at Royal Ascot by the winner

runners.name: Monvoe
runners.spotlight: Twice runner-up in nurseries on AW last autumn; reappeared for back-to-back wins, at Limerick and when running away late over the same trip at Leopardstown; second over an extra furlong at the same venue on next start; back in the winning groove at Limerick; up 5lb and a senior rider replaces a 7lb claimer.
runners.comment: Highly dependable gelding, three wins/three seconds from his last six starts, respected

runners.name: Stars Will Shine
runners.spotlight: Course 7f maiden winner last September; found wanting in a Group 3 race on heavy ground on final start; down the field when one of the main fancies for her handicap debut at this venue; questions to answer.
runners.comment: Down the field when one of the main fancies for her handicap debut at this venue

runners.name: Antigua
runners.spotlight: Modest form at two but shaped better when fourth in maidens at Leopardstown on first two runs this season; 1m4f seemed to stretch him; tongue-tie and blinkers seemed to do the trick in a 1m2f maiden at Fairyhouse; this trip may be a shade shorter than ideal
runners.comment: Tongue-tie and blinkers seemed to do the trick in a 1m2f maiden, trip may be a shade short

runners.name: Liberation Date
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f maiden at Dundalk last December; fair sixth over 1m here last month; 3lb out of the handicap here and looks held by Punica Granatum and Anushka; respectable run here on Friday; needs more.
runners.comment: 3lb wrong, looks held by Punica Granatum and Anushka, okay run here on Friday, needs more

runners.name: Rolltight
runners.spotlight: Ran once at two; winning favourite in a 1m maiden at Gowran on his second start for this stable; has potential for further improvement.
runners.comment: Winning favourite in a 1m maiden at Gowran, has potential for further improvement

runners.name: Sanctijude
runners.spotlight: Consistent in maidens and handicaps before breakthrough win at Gowran on her tenth start; has got away with a 2lb rise but will need to find a little extra; cheekpieces fitted now.
runners.comment: Consistent in maidens and handicaps before breakthrough win at Gowran on her tenth start

runners.name: Manton Bay
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner last year; placed at a decent standard in two turf handicaps this season; not without a chance of reversing Naas form with Bobby McGee on these terms.
runners.comment: AW winner, not without a chance of reversing Naas form with Bobby McGee on these terms

runners.name: Brownstown
runners.spotlight: Kept good company last season; ran in two Pattern races, also contested a strong Listed race and the Goffs Million; ran in a valuable conditions race at Gowran on second outing this term; initially rated 9lb higher than this last year; could be well treated for handicap debut; tongue-tie fitted.
runners.comment: Initially rated 9lb higher than this last year, could be well treated for handicap debut

runners.name: How'sthebai
runners.spotlight: Won a 1m AW maiden in October on last of three juvenile outings; promising seasonal return in 1m Naas premier handicap in late April, staying on well in closing stages, beaten around one length in fourth behind Bobby McGee; saw out this trip when beating excellent yardstick Monvoe at Leopardstown; extra 9lb may anchor him.
runners.comment: Saw out this trip when beating the useful Monvoe at Leopardstown, extra 9lb may anchor him

runners.name: Satoyama
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner over 1m at Gowran on his second start; failed to deliver when well fancied for a 1m conditions event at Listowel; strictly speaking, not well treated relative to Bobby McGee; however, the champion jockey partners him in preference to two others from the stable.
runners.comment: Gowran winner, failed to deliver at Listowel, not well treated relative to Bobby McGee

runners.name: Ryefield Dasher
runners.spotlight: Some promise at two, including in maidens at this venue; beaten 13l when sixth of seven behind Hows'thebai on handicap/seasonal debut; 3lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Some promise at two but was beaten 13l when sixth of seven behind Hows'thebai on return

runners.name: Listentodwindblow
runners.spotlight: Took a high rating into this season after finishing 6 3/4l behind Christmas Day in a Leopardstown Group 3 on final 2yo start; was entitled to win a Naas maiden on Wednesday on his third run this term; probably vulnerable with a 7lb penalty.
runners.comment: Was entitled to win a Naas maiden on Wednesday, probably vulnerable  with a 7lb penalty

races.race_name: Dubai Duty Free Irish EBF Ragusa Handicap (Premier Handicap)

runners.name: In My Teens
runners.spotlight: Down Royal maiden winner last June (10.5f, good); ran well in 1m2f handicap here last October and just 1lb higher here; shaped like coming back to form when keeping on from the back over 1m2f here latest; bred to stay this far; could go well.
runners.comment: Kept on from the back over 1m2f here latest, bred to stay this far, could go well

runners.name: Factual Fact
runners.spotlight: In fine form this term; 1.25l runner-up on seasonal return over C&D (sft-hvy) in March; raised 4lb and won over 2m1f (good) at Killarney; good second off revised mark here on latest (1m6f, yielding); nudged up another 4lb and should run well again, but needs to find a bit more to win off this mark.
runners.comment: In fine form, good second over 1m6f here latest, nudged up 4lb, needs to find a bit more

runners.name: Railwayview Lady
runners.spotlight: Shock 125-1 winner of Navan maiden last season, then placed on handicap debut at Down Royal (1m5f, yielding); caught the eye making late gains over 1m2f here (good) last month in first-time cheekpieces (retained); eased 2lb for that 4.75l defeat (0.75l behind Cloud Seeker) and she's an intriguing runner for her in-form stable.
runners.comment: Made late gains over 1m2f here latest, eased 2lb, intriguing runner upped in trip

runners.name: Star Harbour
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner, including this race off 8lb higher in 2024; no joy last season but back to form with 2.25l second behind subsequent Royal Ascot winner Opportunity in class 2 handicap at Carlisle 29 days ago (1m3f, gd-fm); big chance if building on that run.
runners.comment: Won this in 2024 off 8lb higher, form of latest Carlisle second franked, big player

runners.name: Obscenity
runners.spotlight: In fine fettle this season, winning at Navan (1m2f, gd-yld) off 65 and on latest at Gowran off 78 (1m4f, yielding); hiked up another 9lb and moves up in grade now; unproven on properly fast ground, but this well-bred sort should go well if he acts on it.
runners.comment: Wins at Navan and Gowran this term, quicker ground a query, chance if handles it

runners.name: Marazion
runners.spotlight: 1m4f Roscommon maiden winner (good) shaped promisingly when a 1.5l second of 24 on handicap debut here in May (1m6f, good) off 10lb lower; not disgraced in Cork Group 3 (1m4f, good) on latest, but 6lb rise for that makes things tougher back in handicap company.
runners.comment: Beaten off 10lb lower on handicap debut here last month, okay Gr'3 run latest, needs more

runners.name: Cloud Seeker
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner at 1m2f-1m5f; quick ground fine and was beaten a neck when second in this race last year off 91; just 1lb higher now and shaped nicely enough on return from a break over 1m2f (good) here on latest; will strip fitter now and big player.
runners.comment: Five-time winner, runner up in this last year, sharper for latest run here, big player

runners.name: Navy Waters
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come at Down Royal, the most recent a fillies' handicap (1m5f, yielding) last September off 12lb lower; close fifth off this mark in Cork Derby (1m4f soft) on last Flat start; decent effort over hurdles at Punchestown 22 days ago; good ground fine and place chance back on the level.
runners.comment: Dual Down Royal winner, good run in Cork Derby last month, place shout off same mark

runners.name: The Real Screamer
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner added a second turf win in decisive fashion on return from 107 days off on first try over 1m4f (good) at Leopardstown last month; ridden by 7lb claimer that day so effectively 15lb higher here; will need a big career best to follow up, but unexposed at this distance and can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Easy win on first try at this trip latest, career best needed to follow up, can't discount

runners.name: What's Your Game
runners.spotlight: Won on handicap bow at Cork (1m4f, soft) last season off 14lb lower and then ran well in a Listed heat at Naas when a 3l second behind Carmers (1m5f, good); poor both starts this season, albeit heavy ground may not have suited on latest; could do better back on a sounder surface.
runners.comment: Below best in two runs this year, heavy ground possible excused latest, could do better
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Dawn Rising
runners.spotlight: Smart dual-purpose performer but on losing run since November 2024; excellent second in the Cesarewitch at Newmarket last October but well below best in three runs this term and this trip looks far too sharp; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Smart dual-purpose sort, out of sorts this season, this trip looks far too sharp

runners.name: Retracement
runners.spotlight: Won off 6lb lower here in October (1m2f, yielding); handles quicker ground and should come on for pleasing comeback run over 1m2f here (good) last month, but has to prove he stays this far.
runners.comment: 1m2f win off 6lb lower here in October, sharper for comeback run latest, stamina query

runners.name: Truth Be Told
runners.spotlight: Signed off last season with a win at Thurles (12.5f, gd-yld) for Dermot Weld; followed up off 7lb higher on stable debut and seasonal return at Leopardstown (1m2f, good); up another 7lb now and this is tougher, but the return to 1m4f is fine and he won with a bit in hand last time; can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Gone up 14lb for wins at Thurles and Leopardstown, trip/ground fine, can't discount

runners.name: This Songisforyou
runners.spotlight: No joy since winning over 1m2f here (good) in September 2024; didn't fire last season and no sign of a revival in a maiden hurdle at Clonmel in sole run this year; remains 3lb above last success.
runners.comment: No joy since Sept' 2024, remains 3lb above that win, poor run over hurdles latest

races.race_name: bet365 Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Pony Soprano
runners.spotlight: Has taken his form to a new level since wearing tongue-tie and cheekpieces, comfortably opening his chase account in Uttoxeter event then creditable second at Cartmel, both over 2m5f on good ground; may still have more to offer in the retained headgear; remains of interest.
runners.comment: Course winner on penultimate start and may still have more to offer in retained headgear

runners.name: Tamarind Bay
runners.spotlight: Maiden prior to easily justifying favouritism at Stratford (2m6f, good) on chase/seasonal debut, improving plenty for the change of sphere; defied top weight on that occasion and, despite 8lb rise, is now at the other end of the handicap upped in grade; respected.
runners.comment: Easily justified favouritism at Stratford on chase/seasonal debut and may improve further

runners.name: Sony Bill
runners.spotlight: Showed fairly useful form over hurdles for Willie Mullins without managing to add to his French wins; beaten favourite switched to fences last time (sold for 64,000gns since) but may improve with that experience under his belt and is interesting on debut for Faye Bramley, who has done well with recruits from other yards (including ex-Closutton runners).
runners.comment: Interesting on debut for Faye Bramley, who has done well with recruits from other yards

races.race_name: Never Ordinary At bet365 Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Way Maker
runners.spotlight: Looks suited by good/firmer; dual bumper winner who has been placed in both attempts over hurdles, runner-up at Southwell upped to 2m4f latest; a proper summer type and holds major claims at the weights.
runners.comment: Dual bumper winner; placed in both hurdles attempts; major player at the weights

runners.name: Flying Ace
runners.spotlight: Irish 5yo who is 2-3 since switched to hurdles, the wins on good ground at Cartmel (2m1f) and Perth (2m4f) by an aggregate of 13l; improving and, even under a big penalty, commands respect in hat-trick attempt.
runners.comment: Irish 5yo who is 2-3 since switched to hurdles; improving and commands respect

runners.name: Moi Mon Vieux
runners.spotlight: Has shown promise in bumpers on French and British soil, finishing second twice, last time at Southwell; interesting in receipt of 4yo allowance, assuming he can show progress switched to hurdles.
runners.comment: Runner-up twice in bumpers; interesting assuming he can show progress over hurdles

runners.name: Jetbob
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; 1-5 in points prior to making all for 11l win in 2m4f hunter chase at the Punchestown festival for Sean Doyle last time; sold for £28,000 since; the query is whether he can transfer the form to hurdles.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; made all in hunter chase at Punchestown last time; switches to hurdles

runners.name: Not Now
runners.spotlight: Not solid on his autumn bumper form, having finished third of four at Newton Abbot then well beaten in bigger field at Hereford; chance depends on the effects of first-time headgear and switch to to hurdles.
runners.comment: Not solid on his autumn bumper form; cheekpieces applied

races.race_name: bet365 Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Kingston Narcissus
runners.spotlight: Has taken his form to a new level since switched to fences, winning by wide margin at Hexham (final start for Rebecca Menzies) then creditable second at Worcester, both over 2m4f on good ground; likely to be as effective back down in distance and remains open to further progress in this sphere; respected.
runners.comment: Remains open to further progress over fences; second outing for new stable; respected

runners.name: Coastguard Station
runners.spotlight: Campaigned over longer trips since September but effective at 2m previously; currently in a consistent vein of form and looks attractively treated, having scored twice off current mark last year; landed this contest in 2024 to boot and holds a fighting chance.
runners.comment: Won this contest in 2024 and is on a workable mark back here; fighting chance

runners.name: Gris Majeur
runners.spotlight: Has registered his hurdles/chase wins in Class 4, including comfortably at Bangor (2m1f) last time when looking better than ever; something to prove in this better grade but receives weight all round and could go well in current form.
runners.comment: Looked better than ever last time; upped in class but could go well in current form
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Brian Davies

runners.name: Newtown Rambler
runners.spotlight: Front-running 7yo who brings impressive stats, being 3-5 since joining new yard, 3-4 since dropped to 2m and wearing cheekpieces, 2-2 since returned to chasing (Perth double) and 3-3 under Oakley Brown; should make another bold bid, despite taking a 9lb hike for latest win.
runners.comment: Brings impressive stats, including 3-4 since dropped to 2m and wearing cheekpieces

runners.name: Breaking Cover
runners.spotlight: Faced a stiff task on unsuitably soft ground when last seen; progressive otherwise and ran well in Grade 2 novice chase at Wincanton earlier in November; interesting with Sam Twiston-Davies back on board, having been successful in five of his last six starts (fences/hurdles) under this rider.
runners.comment: Broadly progressive; successful in five of his last six starts under Sam Twiston-Davies

runners.name: Sir Tivo
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner down the years and latest effort suggests he retains plenty of ability aged 12; has a respectable record (252, last three years) in this corresponding race but it remains to be seen whether his old legs can make it fourth time lucky.
runners.comment: Veteran who has a respectable record (252) in this corresponding race

races.race_name: bet365 Summer Cup Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Grand Clermont
runners.spotlight: Ex-French; technically a veteran, being a 10yo, but he's still unexposed in Britain and looks a suitable type for this prize, having scored by a wide margin over C&D (good) on penultimate outing; ran well behind Samuel Spade at Worcester latest; possibilities.
runners.comment: Still unexposed in Britain; scored by wide margin over C&D on penultimate start

runners.name: Hang In There
runners.spotlight: Gained most recent success in a veterans' event; two attempts at this prize comprise a success (off 9lb higher) in 2024 and below-par seventh last year; the 12yo's chance of regaining the Cup partly depends on the effects of first-time visor.
runners.comment: Two attempts at this prize comprise a win in 2024 and below-par seventh last year

runners.name: Samuel Spade
runners.spotlight: Lacked fluency on chase debut but jumped better and beat Grand Clermont kept to Worcester (2m7f, good) last time; unexposed in this sphere and remains favourably treated on best hurdles form, which features a win off 1lb higher; major claims.
runners.comment: Won at Worcester last time; unexposed over fences and still favourably treated

runners.name: Riskintheground
runners.spotlight: Successful on this card two years ago; campaigned mostly over 2m4f/2m5f but saw out 3m well at Perth (good) most recently, adding to his Grade 2 handicap success in April and taking chase record to 9-30; better than ever and commands respect.
runners.comment: Raced mainly over shorter but saw out 3m well at Perth last time; better than ever

runners.name: Queensbury Boy
runners.spotlight: Novice chaser whose form this term comprises a walkover at Hereford and close second at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good); bred to be suited by a test of stamina and he is still unexposed over 3m; interesting back up in distance, with progress still plausible.
runners.comment: Novice chaser who is still unexposed over 3m; interesting back up in distance

runners.name: Twig
runners.spotlight: Gamely landed the Becher Chase in December to cement a cracking start to last season; not in quite the same form since but his Uttoxeter record bodes well; record of 2-3 here features a win (albeit off 9lb lower as an 8yo) in this race in 2023.
runners.comment: Record of 2-3 at Uttoxeter features a success in this race in 2023

runners.name: Alien Storm
runners.spotlight: Successful five times at up to 2m4f, including twice last autumn; clear second upped to 3m at Bangor (good) most recently, running at least as well as he's ever done; may remain competitive despite his new mark being a career-high one.
runners.comment: Clear second upped to 3m at Bangor last time; may remain competitive

runners.name: Time To Bite
runners.spotlight: Chase record is 9-35, the most recent win in veterans' event at Fakenham (3m, good) on penultimate start; consistent sort but faces a stiffer task off current mark in this valuable and competitive affair; similar type to Hold Your Fort.
runners.comment: Consistent sort but faces a stiffer task in this competitive affair

runners.name: Regarde
runners.spotlight: Stays well, as his two performances over 3m4f at Haydock attest; finished well for second over 3m1f at Aintree (good) most recently; not crying out for this drop back to 3m but may be aided by new headgear; represents last year's winning yard.
runners.comment: Not crying out for the drop back to 3m but may be aided by new headgear

runners.name: Ki Woo
runners.spotlight: Proving a strong stayer, with recent form featuring wins over 3m4f at Haydock (good to soft) and an extended 3m1f at Cartmel (good); doesn't seem a benefactor of the decision to reduce this race's distance to 3m; otherwise open to further progress over fences.
runners.comment: May ideally need further now but otherwise open to further progress over fences

runners.name: Hold Your Fort
runners.spotlight: Chase record is 7-20, the most recent success in veterans' contest at Ascot (3m, good) in March; couple of sound efforts since demonstrate that he remains in form aged ten; faces a tougher assignment off current mark in this field, however.
runners.comment: Remains in form but faces a tougher assignment in this field

runners.name: Peaky Boy
runners.spotlight: Steadily coming back to form since joining current stable, as a couple of third-place finishes this season attest; was a useful/progressive sort earlier in career and looks interesting off a reduced mark, assuming his resurgence is sustained.
runners.comment: Interesting off a reduced mark, assuming his resurgence is sustained

runners.name: Imperial Alex
runners.spotlight: Back in top form this season; justified favouritism at Stratford (3m3f, good to firm) last time, taking chase record to 5-19; however, the memory of his dismal effort in this contest two years ago (when bidding for a four-timer) tempers enthusiasm.
runners.comment: Back in top form but his dismal effort in this race in 2024 tempers enthusiasm

races.race_name: bet365 Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: American Sniper
runners.spotlight: Made all for 13l success at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good) last time, ending a losing run that went back to 2024; still competitively handicapped on historical data and has won at up to 3m; not dismissed.
runners.comment: Made all at Newton Abbot most recently; still competitively handicapped

runners.name: Miss Goldfire
runners.spotlight: Successful in both completed starts this term, latest in ready fashion against her own sex at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good); on a career-high mark and goes back against males but arrives in top form.
runners.comment: Successful in both completed starts this term; on a tougher mark but arrives in top form
runners.owner: Harry Fry Racing Syndicate Miss Goldfire

runners.name: Oakley
runners.spotlight: Generally consistent at up to 2m4f for current yard, winning twice, both times at Hereford; Flat record provides optimism that he'll stay this new trip; whether he'll cope with the rise in class is another matter.
runners.comment: Worth a crack at this new trip but needs to prove he can cope with this grade

runners.name: My Gift To You
runners.spotlight: Successful when last seen over hurdles but this looks a tougher assignment and his most recent win (penultimate start) was achieved in a 4m1f hunter chase; others preferred.
runners.comment: Gained most recent win in 4m1f hunter chase; others preferred

runners.name: Crebilly
runners.spotlight: Got up in final stride in second-time cheekpieces at Market Rasen (2m4f, good) most recently; remains well treated on peak form and this return to further is worth exploring; enters calculations in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Got up in final stride over 2m4f last time; remains well treated on peak form

runners.name: Escapologist
runners.spotlight: 2m7f winner at Market Rasen in March for previous yard and not disgraced at Plumpton on stable debut last time; had wind surgery since and now goes in tongue-tie, with cheekpieces reapplied; interesting, with a bigger effort plausible.
runners.comment: Had wind surgery since latest start (stable debut); bigger effort looks plausible

runners.name: Musique De Fee
runners.spotlight: Scored five times for previous stable; 0-6 for current yard but has posted a couple of encouraging efforts and the effects of a busy spell likely explain her last two runs; rested 32 days since; possibilities under James Bowen.
runners.comment: Bits of encouraging form for current yard; last two efforts are excusable; possibilities

runners.name: Blizzard Of Oz
runners.spotlight: Well treated on best chase form for Willie Mullins and is a three-time hurdles winner; sold for £39,000 since last run; interesting on debut for Faye Bramley, who has done well with recruits from other stables (including ex-Closutton runners).
runners.comment: Interesting on debut for Faye Bramley, who has done well with recruits from other stables

runners.name: Ebony Warrior
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism with 3l success at Worcester (2m7f, good) last time, bouncing back to form and taking hurdles record to 3-12; reassessment leaves him back on the peak mark he had for a while in 2024 and 2025; may remain competitive.
runners.comment: Justified favouritism at Worcester last time, bouncing back to form
runners.owner: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson

runners.name: Bold Endeavour
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on best form for previous yards but needs to stage a revival on debut for Chris Honour; resurgent efforts in January/March have not been built on; sold for £23,000 since last run; market may offer further guidance.
runners.comment: Needs to stage a revival on debut for new stable; market may offer further guidance

runners.name: Gabbys Cross
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly in veterans' chases during his spell with Nick Scholfield and this return to hurdles isn't guaranteed to produce a positive result on debut for another new stable; hasn't won since 2022.
runners.comment: Return to hurdles isn't guaranteed to produce a positive result on stable debut
runners.owner: J P McGrath & R N Pallas

races.race_name: Bet Boost At bet365 Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Edelak
runners.spotlight: Flat winner in Ireland and successful in last two attempts over hurdles, latest in a canter against two rivals at Stratford (2m, good) on handicap debut; respected up just 7lb, with further improvement likely.
runners.comment: Successful in last two starts, latest in a canter on handicap debut; respected up just 7lb

runners.name: Caelan
runners.spotlight: Proving reasonably consistent, having made the frame in most starts since winning a maiden event last September; a possible danger off current mark with Lewis Saunders taking off a bonus 5lb.
runners.comment: Looks a possible danger off current mark with Lewis Saunders taking off a bonus 5lb

runners.name: Hope Rising
runners.spotlight: Comes with some risk, having refused to race on one occasion and lost many lengths by whipping around at the start last time (ran well in the circumstances); otherwise capable of playing a big role.
runners.comment: Comes with risk (can be tricky at the start) but otherwise capable of playing a big role

races.race_name: Best Odds Guaranteed At bet365 Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Hello Cello
runners.spotlight: Usually ridden by Ciaran O'Shea; breakthrough win in 2m3f handicap at Fontwell last October and back-to-form third of 12 at Warwick (2m5f, good) early last month; in serious contention if that form can be maintained.
runners.comment: Won last October and back-to-form third of 12 at Warwick (2m5f, good) early last month
runners.owner: Harry Fry Racing Syndicate Hello Cello

runners.name: Dream's Ka
runners.spotlight: Off the mark over hurdles at seventh attempt, with ready success at Bangor (near 2m4f, good) in May; good second of five at Warwick (2m3f, good) this month, which sees him another 3lb higher today but he's in the mix.
runners.comment: Won at Bangor (near 2m4f) in May; good second of five at Warwick (2m3f, good) this month

runners.name: Kom Tu Voudras
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start for Faye Bramley in 2m5f Huntingdon maiden (heavy) in February; built on that in good ground-handicaps at Warwick and Worcester, latterly when making all to justify favouritism in first-time cheekpieces; up 4lb but lightly raced and going the right way.
runners.comment: 121 for yard; made all in first cheekpieces latest; lightly raced and going the right way

runners.name: Gardener
runners.spotlight: 10yo who had two wins at Hexham (soft/good) this spring before a backward step when bidding to complete his hat-trick over that 2m4f; ridden by Charlie Maggs on last four starts; each-way candidate.
runners.comment: Two wins at Hexham before a backward step when bidding to complete 2m4f hat-trick there
runners.owner: Mrs J M Plummer & M E Sowersby

runners.name: Ballygeary
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent 9yo who is falling in the weights (17lb below peak 2024 mark) but his last piece of persuasive form was in July last year.
runners.comment: Falling in the weights but his last piece of persuasive form was in July last year

runners.name: Ocean Conquest
runners.spotlight: Completed a good-ground double at Ludlow (2m novice and 2m5f handicap) last autumn; has sparked only once (two runs back) from four starts since; perhaps 3m was too far in two of those but others are still preferred.
runners.comment: Sparked once (two runs back) in last four starts; perhaps 3m was too far in two of those

runners.name: Suttons Hill
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles (last seen in this sphere in 2024) in Ireland and 1-14 in points; has changed hands and left Thomas Britten; chance rests on form in 2023.
runners.comment: 0-8 as hurdler in Ireland and 1-14 in points; has changed hands; chance rests on 2023 form

races.race_name: Molson Coors Mares' Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Cinating
runners.spotlight: Confirmed previous promise when winning a Roscommon maiden hurdle last month (2m, good to yielding; made all) in a first-time hood (on again); contender on that form.
runners.comment: Off the mark when making all at Roscommon last month (first-time hood); contender

runners.name: Eremenko
runners.spotlight: Bought for £8,000 out of Nicky Henderson's yard last month having won a Plumpton maiden hurdle in April (2m4f, good; second won next time) despite making a bad mistake two out; excuses for poor run next time; interesting down in trip on stable debut.
runners.comment: £8,000 buy out of Nicky Henderson's yard in May having won a Plumpton maiden; stable debut

runners.name: Crokes Cross
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; well beaten in two bumpers but off the mark on stable/hurdles debut in a Kelso novice (2m, good to soft) at 300-1 in April; that form is fair, but stiffer task with her penalty.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; won Kelso novice at 300-1 in April on stable/hurdles debut; stiffer task here

runners.name: Millena Agent
runners.spotlight: GB point winner last November (2m4f, heavy); well beaten both hurdle starts; hood now tried; looks one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Point winner in November but well beaten both hurdle starts; looks one for handicaps

runners.name: Rachel Lopez
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden pointer; 17l second at Hexham on stable/hurdles debut (2m, good to soft) but hasn't run as well on either start since; bit to prove.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; maiden pointer; fair 2nd on hurdles/stable debut; poor form since; bit to prove

runners.name: Letmeseethecolts
runners.spotlight: Won a Pontefract handicap on Flat in April 2025 (1m4f, good; modest form); hasn't run as well since, including on both starts this year following a 380-day break; hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Won 1m4f handicap last year but below par in 2026 after 380-day break; hurdle debut

runners.name: Order Another One
runners.spotlight: Bought for 30,000euros last month having won a mares' maiden on her seventh start pointing in Ireland; stable/hurdles debut.
runners.comment: 30,000euros buy after winning a mares' maiden on 7th start pointing; stable/hurdles debut

runners.name: Big Gypsy King
runners.spotlight: Modest Flat performer; well beaten all three runs over hurdles; plenty to find.
runners.comment: Modest Flat performer; well beaten all three runs over hurdles; plenty to find

runners.name: Echinacea
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden; fair on the Flat and improved on her first run over hurdles when 12.5l fourth at Downpatrick last time (2m3f, good to yielding); each-way chance.
runners.comment: 16-race maiden; improved on debut when 4th at Downpatrick latest (2m3f); each-way chance

runners.name: Raincloud
runners.spotlight: Can be troublesome at the start; longstanding maiden who is hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Can be troublesome at the start; longstanding maiden who is hard to fancy

races.race_name: Burlington Stone Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Roi Du Roume
runners.spotlight: Irish maiden point winner; the subject of a foiled gamble when only fourth of six finishers at Perth (2m4f, good) in April; produced a lacklustre display when last of four finishers on his chase debut at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good) last month and not the most fluent but stayed on well when second over fences here on Friday in tongue-tie and cheekpieces, both first time (retained); may be worth another shot back over hurdles.
runners.comment: Gamble foiled when only fourth at Perth in April; second over fences here on Friday.

runners.name: Masterminding
runners.spotlight: 0-15 under rules but showed improved form on his stable debut when runner-up over C&D (good) last month, following five months off; should come on from that outing and run well.
runners.comment: Showed improved form on his stable debut when runner-up over C&D last month.

runners.name: Path Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 15th attempt over hurdles, over C&D (good) last month; 4lb higher but should give a good account in her follow-up bid.
runners.comment: Off the mark at the 15th attempt over hurdles, over C&D last month; 4lb higher.

runners.name: Seeyainthesuninn
runners.spotlight: Has shown bits of form that give him claims, such as when third at Perth (2m4f, good) in April, but pulled up at Hexham (2m7f, good) last month; his mark continues to fall but he needs to show more consistency.
runners.comment: Third at Perth in April but pulled up at Hexham last month.

runners.name: Ballynoe Boy
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame twice over hurdles and ran as though the run was needed when seventh of 12 at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good) 15 days ago following a six-month break; steps up in trip and has dropped to a career-low mark; should not be far away.
runners.comment: Ran as though the run was needed when seventh at Uttoxeter 15 days ago.

runners.name: Fine As Wine
runners.spotlight: 0-6 but ran well to finish third in handicaps over 2m4f on good at Southwell and Market Rasen in the spring; she should be suited by the step up in trip and Sean Bowen rides for the first time; should not be far away.
runners.comment: Third in handicaps over 2m4f on good at Southwell and Market Rasen in the spring.

runners.name: Imperial Rule
runners.spotlight: This track (2m5f, good) last month saw him land his fourth race over fences, following a short break; resumes over hurdles where he is 10lb lower but yet to make the frame in this sphere; claims if he can transfer his chase form.
runners.comment: This track last month saw him land his fourth race over fences, following a short break.

runners.name: Kantagua Du Large
runners.spotlight: Ex-French mare still searching for her first win in Britain; beaten 36l when favourite at Warwick (3m1f, good) last month; her mark continues to fall but she has it all to prove.
runners.comment: Beaten 36l when favourite at Warwick last month.

runners.name: Caspada
runners.spotlight: Best run when second in a Chepstow maiden (2m, good; 150-1) in April; sixth of ten to complete at Southwell (2m, good) on his handicap debut 19 days ago; steps up in trip but looks a tad high in the weights.
runners.comment: Best run when second in a Chepstow maiden (2m, good; 150-1) in April.

runners.name: Poetry Man
runners.spotlight: Scored over fences in December 2024 but pulled up in that sphere in three consecutive races this year; resumed over hurdles last month with two solid efforts when third twice at this track, once over today's distance in a visor (retained); races prominently but needs his natural enthusiasm to be harnessed.
runners.comment: Resumed over hurdles last month with two solid efforts when third at this track.

races.race_name: Campbell & Rowley Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Sea The Clouds
runners.spotlight: Won here over C&D in July 2024 and over fences in July 2025 (his only wins in those years); the second of his two races this May (both under Leah Noreci) suggests he could be building up to a good run; first-time visor replaces his usual cheekpieces; stable runs three.
runners.comment: Latest race suggests he could be building up to a good run; first-time visor; yard runs 3

runners.name: Rauzan
runners.spotlight: Two placings this spring give him a chance but they were followed by two lesser efforts and latest was C&D when tongue tied first time since 2024; visor (used twice in 2025) returns instead of usual cheekpieces; stable runs two.
runners.comment: Two placings this spring give him a chance but they were followed by two lesser efforts

runners.name: Miss Manzor
runners.spotlight: Useful as juvenile hurdler for Willie Mullins but didn't take to fences; three recent runs for Gordon Elliott haven't been great, but not ruled out.
runners.comment: Three recent runs for her new trainer Gordon Elliott haven't been great, but not ruled out

runners.name: Secret Secret
runners.spotlight: Three wins over 2m6f from ten races at Cartmel, all on softer than good; tends to pop up once a year and latest run was a return to near his best; considered despite being 5lb above his highest winning mark; yard runs three.
runners.comment: Three 2m6f wins from ten races at Cartmel, all on softer than good but he's considered

runners.name: Nakassama
runners.spotlight: Last two visits to Britain resulted in a win at Hexham (2m4f, good to soft) last June and a narrow loss at Ayr (2m, soft) this March; won well at Downpatrick (2m6f, good) 15 days ago and all three of those runs were under Oran McGill; needs plenty of respect despite his steeply revised mark.
runners.comment: Won well at Downpatrick (2m6f, good) 15 days ago; respected despite a steeply revised mark

runners.name: Feach Amach
runners.spotlight: Three wins last spring and a career best to score at Musselburgh (2m4f, good to soft) this March, albeit with half the hurdles omitted; ran creditably at Kelso (2m5f, good) 12 weeks ago but that fourth of six suggests that she needs to raise her game again.
runners.comment: In career-best form but fourth of six last time suggests she needs to raise her game again

runners.name: Divilabother
runners.spotlight: Stable is going well at the moment and this 8yo contributed with a clearcut win at Hexham (extended 2m7f, good) 15 days ago; up 5lb today; usually ridden in the last ten months by Harry Reed, who's on stablemate Pyramid Place this time but, on the face of it, this is the yard's stronger option.
runners.comment: Clearcut win at Hexham (extended 2m7f, good) 15 days ago; up 5lb today

runners.name: It's All About You
runners.spotlight: Didn't set the world alight in his three handicap hurdles (won his two previous hurdle starts); those handicaps were at up to 2m1f and he's much more of a stayer on the Flat, but his two Flat runs at Chester (where he won last season) this term were disappointing; tongue tied first time.
runners.comment: New hurdles trip is interesting but both 2026 Flat runs were poor; tongue tied first time
runners.owner: A Baxter, S, Sowray, K Ward

runners.name: Evenwood Sonofagun
runners.spotlight: Yard also runs Rauzan but this one has three C&D wins from six visits here; latest win was last month and he went close under 7lb penalty over 3m1f here two days later; those were his only two runs since latest wind surgery; raised another 4lb today but he has to be on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Three C&D wins from six visits here and a close call over 3m1f here on latest outing
runners.owner: Mrs D Chapman & Mrs E Quinlan

runners.name: Heart Above
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in his only two races at Cartmel; short-head win at Kelso (2m5f, good) five weeks ago, which seems his favourite track but he needs respect with that return to form.
runners.comment: Made frame in his two races at Cartmel; short-head win at Kelso (2m5f) five weeks ago

runners.name: Dinons
runners.spotlight: Usually ridden these days by Joshua Thompson; 13yo whose last win came from 15lb higher at Uttoxeter (3m, good) in July 2024; tailed off last May in his only Cartmel race and although some minor honours since, they were in smaller fields than this and today's race is probably a bit too competitive.
runners.comment: 3yo whose last win was in July 2024; today's race is probably a bit too competitive

runners.name: Pyramid Place
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler in his prime but out of form for a good while now, including on this month's two runs (latest a rare Flat run) for new stable; it's therefore hard to see him adding to the trainer's recent winners; Divilabother looks a much better chance for the yard but Harry Reed (who rides most of their British runners) is on this one.
runners.comment: Divilabother looks a much better chance for the yard but Harry Reed is on this one

races.race_name: Oakmere Homes Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Goodwin Face
runners.spotlight: In good form at present, following up last month's Plumpton win (2m, good) with a wide-margin C&D success on Friday; penalised for that, and this a stronger race, but one to consider.
runners.comment: Followed up May's Plumpton win with wide-margin C&D success on Friday; should go well

runners.name: Nap Hand
runners.spotlight: Formerly with Alan King; creditable second at Warwick on latest hurdles start (2m, good) and has since finished third in a 1m4f handicap at Salisbury; stable debut; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Ex-Alan King; creditable 2nd at Warwick last time; each-way chance on stable debut

runners.name: Bittalemon
runners.spotlight: Both hurdle wins were in novice company over C&D last summer; best of two runs since wind surgery when third over 2m6f here last time (May); interesting.
runners.comment: Both hurdles wins were over C&D last summer; good run over 2m6f here latest; interesting

runners.name: High Court Cave
runners.spotlight: Won maiden hurdle at Downpatrick in September (2m2f, yielding); fair form in two handicaps since; interesting with cheekpieces now tried.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner; fair form in two runs since; interesting with cheekpieces tried

runners.name: Fiveonefive
runners.spotlight: 3-3 here, having won this race for the last three years (good/good to soft); hasn't been in much form this year, but is 2lb lower than for last season's win and one to consider.
runners.comment: 3-3 here, having won this race for last 3 years; 2lb lower than in 2025; one to consider

runners.name: Miss Maverick
runners.spotlight: 6-16 over hurdles, her latest success being at Uttoxeter in May (2m4f, good); one of her best runs was when 2l second to Fiveonefive in this race last year, but she is now 6lb worse off with the winner.
runners.comment: 6-16 over hurdles; second to Fiveonefive in this race last year, but now 6lb worse off

runners.name: Amancio
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner at around this trip last year (Chepstow maiden and Sedgefield novice); comes into this having won a Carlisle handicap on the Flat three weeks ago (1m1f, soft); quite interesting.
runners.comment: Dual hurdle winner; comes into this having won on the Flat at Carlisle last time; chance

runners.name: Zillow
runners.spotlight: 24,000gns buy out of Willie Mullins' yard this month; dual hurdle/Flat winner, latest a Listed Fairyhouse handicap hurdle in November (2m, yielding to soft); has not done quite so well since; stable debut.
runners.comment: 24,000gns buy from Willie Mullins; dual hurdle winner, latest a Listed h'cap; stable debut

runners.name: Filibustering
runners.spotlight: Has done well since being tried in a tongue-tie; bids for a hat-trick after scoring at Newcastle in April (2m1f, good) and Hexham last month (2m, good); up 11lb in the process (7lb since latest win); a possible.
runners.comment: Improved with tongue-tie and bids for hat-trick after wins at Newcastle and Hexham; chance

runners.name: Winds Of Winter
runners.spotlight: Won three races last season, two of them over C&D (first a selling handicap); looked on the way back when third of five at Hexham last week; this looks a stronger race.
runners.comment: Two of her three wins have been over C&D; third at Hexham last week but this is tougher

races.race_name: Oakmere Homes Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Got Grey
runners.spotlight: Won twice over hurdles and once over fences for Dan Skelton; sold to join current yard for £10,000 in October; not beaten far when last of three at this track (2m1f, good) last month on stable debut following seven-month break and should be sharper this time; not out of this.
runners.comment: Not beaten far when last of three at this track last month on stable debut.

runners.name: Light N Strike
runners.spotlight: Won six races (five over fences) for Emma Lavelle; yet to fire in five starts for current yard; first-time blinkers need to work for him to rediscover his old form.
runners.comment: Won six races for Emma Lavelle; yet to fire in five runs for current yard; first blinkers

runners.name: Buy Some Time
runners.spotlight: Won three over hurdles and got off the mark as chaser on resuming over fences at Perth (3m, good) three weeks ago; while a 7lb rise is not too harsh, he will need to show more improvement in this higher grade.
runners.comment: Won three over hurdles and got off the mark over fences at Perth three weeks ago.

runners.name: Sean Og
