runners.spotlight: All five wins over fences have been at this track, four over today's distance; at his best when scoring over C&D (good) last month and 4lb rise still gives him a good chance.
runners.comment: All five wins over fences have been at this track, four at today's distance; good chance.

runners.name: Pour Les Filles
runners.spotlight: A winning hurdler for Gordon Elliott in Ireland, he made a successful handicap chase debut for this yard at Ffos Las (2m3f, soft) in January; reportedly hard to keep right, but he returned to form after three lesser efforts when third of five in returning cheekpieces (retained) over C&D (good) last month; first-time tongue-tie is also applied and he holds claims.
runners.comment: Made a winning chase debut at Ffos Las in January; good third over C&D last month.

runners.name: Breizh River
runners.spotlight: In fine fettle in recent runs, landing his fifth win over fences at Kelso (2m5f, good) in April and placed since at Aintree and Perth; swaps cheekpieces for a first-time visor and looks sure to give another good account.
runners.comment: Fifth win over fences at Kelso in April and he has since been placed at Aintree and Perth.

races.race_name: Holker Hall & Gardens Handicap Chase

runners.name: Halfway House Lad
runners.spotlight: 2-2 since joining James Moffatt, latest win over C&D last month (good); up another 7lb (15lb rise in total) but should go well again.
runners.comment: 2-2 for current yard, 2nd win over C&D last month; up another 7lb but should go well again

runners.name: Kingofthegame
runners.spotlight: Broke his duck when winning a 3m1f handicap hurdle here in May (good) having finished third in a 2m5f handicap chase here four days earlier (good); may have more to offer over this trip; well treated over fences; player.
runners.comment: 3m1f hurdles win here in May (3rd over fences here four days earlier); good mark; player

runners.name: Judicial Review
runners.spotlight: Three of his four wins have been here, including in this race off 4lb lower last season; fair run when sixth here in May; disappointing when tailed off here on Friday (2m5f, good) but he twice bounced back last summer after two-day breaks.
runners.comment: Two of four wins were here (won this in 2025); tailed off on Friday; bounced back before

runners.name: Aill Dubh
runners.spotlight: Newcastle winner in March (2m7f, good to soft) who was a fair fourth of eight at Perth last time (3m, good to soft); a possible.
runners.comment: Newcastle winner in March who was a fair fourth at Perth last time; a possible

runners.name: Lights Are Green
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner; creditable third to Halfway House over C&D in May but pulled up after being reluctant to race at Hexham last time.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; good 3rd to Halfway House over C&D in May; reluctant to race last time

runners.name: Shantou Show
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner but last win was a Tipperary novice hunter chase in May 2023 (3m, good to yielding; first-time tongue-tie); on a good mark and there was a more encouraging run last time.
runners.comment: Dual chase winner (last win was back in May 2023); on a good mark and better run last time

runners.name: You Did
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles at Hexham (2m4f, good) and Musselburgh (3m, soft ) last season; below par last two outings and goes chasing after a 143-day break.
runners.comment: Two hurdle wins last season; below par last two starts; goes chasing after 143-day break

runners.name: Star Turn
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden; pulled up on only chase start, in February 2025 (2m2f, soft); comes here having run fair races over hurdles with a visor on last two starts; a possible back chasing with blinkers now tried.
runners.comment: 0-13; fair form with visor over hurdles; pulled up on sole chase run; blinkers now tried

runners.name: Blazing Court
runners.spotlight: Won a Worcester handicap chase for Martin Keighley in August 2024 (2m4f, good) but has a bit to prove having been out of form on most starts for this yard (unseated rider last twice).
runners.comment: Worcester winner for Martin Keighley in 2024; not in much form since; bit to prove

runners.name: Croagh Patrick
runners.spotlight: Not the easiest to win with (has won three times but second on 12 occasions); good run when second to an in-form horse at Hexham last time; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Not the easiest to win with; good 2nd to in-form one at Hexham last time; each-way chance

races.race_name: Women's Community Matters Handicap Chase

runners.name: Dexter
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D last August off 1lb lower; may have needed the run when fourth of six finishers at Bangor (2m1f, good) last month following six-month break and holds claims.
runners.comment: May have needed the run when fourth of six finishers at Bangor last month.

runners.name: Conquer The Breeze
runners.spotlight: Has been in tremendous form at Hexham this summer, landing his fourth course win there last Sunday; incurs a 7lb rise and needs to show he can win away from his favoured venue.
runners.comment: Landed his fourth course win at Hexham last Sunday

runners.name: Ghasham
runners.spotlight: An infrequent winner but got off the mark over fences, after two placed efforts, at Hexham (1m7f, good) 15 days ago; cannot be dismissed while in this mood.
runners.comment: Got off the mark over fences, after two placed efforts, at Hexham 15 days ago.

runners.name: Dream Jet
runners.spotlight: Has three wins over fences but beaten a total of 70l on his two runs once resuming last month; has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Has been beaten a combination of 70l on his two runs once resuming last month.

runners.name: Clean Getaway
runners.spotlight: At his best at Sedgefield where he boasts five wins; fair effort when third to Zumbi over C&D last month but it may be best to wait until he returns to his favoured venue.
runners.comment: Fair effort when third to Zumbi over C&D last month; five wins at Sedgefield.

runners.name: Intenzo
runners.spotlight: 0-9 but ran up to his best at Market Rasen (2m1f, good) last month when runner-up for second time over fences; first-time tongue-tie and hood are now on and he just needs to find that little bit extra to gain his first win.
runners.comment: Runner-up for the second time over fences at Market Rasen last month.

runners.name: Fort Randall
runners.spotlight: Landed a gamble over hurdles at Kilbeggan (2m3f, good to yielding) in April following a 458-day absence and ran well when third off 14lb higher mark there last month; showed little in three runs over fences in 2024 for previous trainer but may be a different proposition these days; market watch advised.
runners.comment: Landed a gamble over hurdles at Kilbeggan in April; third back there last time.

runners.name: Blue Reed
runners.spotlight: Irish raider who didn't fare too well in four attempts over fences previously, but got off the mark over hurdles at Kilbeggan (2m3f, good to yielding) last month at the 28th attempt under rules; not an obvious candidate to follow up, over fences.
runners.comment: Got off the mark over hurdles at the 28th attempt under rules at Kilbeggan last month.

runners.name: Bentley Road
runners.spotlight: Previously campaigned in hunter chases and points, he made an encouraging handicap debut when second to Ghasham at Hexham (1m7f, good) 15 days ago; may appreciate a longer trip.
runners.comment: Encouraging handicap debut when second to Ghasham at Hexham 15 days ago.

races.race_name: Enjoy A Bostin Day At The Races With SFRacing Club Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB)

runners.name: Blue Celestial
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden after eight races but her best AW form (placed in 7f/1m handicaps on Kempton Polytrack) is very solid compared to most of what's on offer among her opponents; has further to go but probably bang there.
runners.comment: Best AW form (placed in 7f/1m handicaps) is very solid compared to most of what's on offer

runners.name: Sunshine And Roses
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden who was always behind on turf at Windsor on seasonal debut; however, she had several bits of AW form last year (at up to 8.6f, most of it here) which put her in the hunt for this prize.
runners.comment: Several bits of AW form last year (at up to 8.6f, most of it here) put her in the hunt

runners.name: Cherringham
runners.spotlight: Sister to 1m2f winner Mighty Quiet (RPR 78); 13-2, looked in need of the experience when fifth of eight on her debut in a maiden at Lingfield (1m2f, soft) three weeks ago; modest form but should improve.
runners.comment: 13-2, in need of the experience when fifth of eight at Lingfield (1m2f, soft) this month

runners.name: Salamis Bay
runners.spotlight: Modest form over 6f on AW on first two starts, before last of seven at Ascot (1m2f, good; 125-1) and well beaten on handicap debut at Yarmouth (11.4f, soft; 33-1); not totally out of it given return to AW for weak race.
runners.comment: Not totally out of it given the return to AW for weak race

runners.name: Sweet Horizon
runners.spotlight: 26,000euros yearling by Lope Y Fernandez; fourth foal; half-sister to winners Mostly Sunny (1m4f/2m Flat including AW, RPR 88; 2m/2m4f hurdle) and Pacaya (Italian 7f 2yo); dam unraced sister to Listed-placed 1m winner and half-sister to 1m4f Grade 3 winner; market can guide on debut in weak race.
runners.comment: 26,000euros yearling by Lope Y Fernandez; market can guide on debut in weak race

runners.name: Ice Show
runners.spotlight: Hooded when well beaten on her first three starts (1m, 1m1f and 7f); her dam was useful and this could be a weak race but she lacks solid claims.
runners.comment: Hooded when well beaten on her first three starts (1m, 1m1f and 7f); her dam was useful

runners.name: Peace Belle
runners.spotlight: Won at Hexham last August in the first of her three bumpers but a Grade 2 at Aintree (125-1; always behind) proved far too tough in April on final outing; a 9.4f maiden may not look the most promising fit at first glance for her first venture under Flat rules but this looks a weak race.
runners.comment: Bumper winner who makes her first venture under Flat rules in what looks a weak race

runners.name: Miss Mambo
runners.spotlight: Well held in handicaps at Ripon (1m, good) and Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW) on last two outings but she had some modest form going into those; blinkers are enlisted; not totally out of calculations.
runners.comment: Modest form before well held in her two handicaps (1m/1m4f); blinkers are enlisted

runners.name: Crack Of Thunder
runners.spotlight: Well-backed 5-1 for a 14-runner bumper at Worcester (2m, good) 17 days ago but tailed off; Flat bred but best watched for now.
runners.comment: Well-backed 5-1 for a bumper at Worcester (2m, good) 17 days ago but tailed off; Flat bred

runners.name: Midnight Media
runners.spotlight: Five races, with two bits of modest form at huge prices over about 1m on Polytrack/Tapeta this spring before no show over 11.4f on her turf/handicap debut; place squeak.
runners.comment: Two bits of modest AW form before no show over 11.4f on her turf/handicap debut

runners.name: Hauteluce
runners.spotlight: 25-1 when well held in mid-division for a Lingfield maiden (1m, AW) in December but there was some promise; there's a lot of stamina on the dam's side, so a fair bit further should her to best effect, but she needs a market check.
runners.comment: 25-1, well held in mid-division for Lingfield maiden (1m, AW) in December but some promise

races.race_name: First Windsorlot Then Voltaic & Now Teardrops Handicap

runners.name: Just A Gambler
runners.spotlight: Made a successful seasonal/handicap debut at Chelmsford (1m, Polytrack) in March, but his form has rather regressed since; 2lb lower than for the win, but others still preferred.
runners.comment: Form has rather regressed since his successful reappearance; others preferred

runners.name: Faeberon
runners.spotlight: Best effort when third of 11 on handicap debut at Thirsk (1m4f, good; first start since being gelded) last month; drop in trip may be no bad thing and cheekpieces are given a go; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Third on handicap debut at Thirsk last month; shorter trip no bad thing; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Magician Of Riga
runners.spotlight: Bids for a hat-trick after wins here (1m4f) and at Southwell (1m3f, Tapeta) in April; 3lb higher mark not the end of the world, but the drop back in trip does pose a question.
runners.comment: Bids for a Tapeta hat-trick off a 3lb higher mark; the drop back in trip poses a question

runners.name: Silky Lass
runners.spotlight: Has proved most consistent on Tapeta and probably ran into one (successful again since) when beaten 7l into second of 13 in an amateurs' event here (8.6f) 12 days ago; ran well over this C&D the time before and likely to run another big race.
runners.comment: Has proved most consistent on Tapeta (5231332); likely to run another big race

runners.name: Windbreaker
runners.spotlight: Improved a little on previous efforts when fourth of nine on handicap debut at Lingfield (1m2f, good) ten days ago; 1lb lower, but he probably needs to progress again.
runners.comment: Fourth of nine on handicap debut at Lingfield ten days ago; needs to progress again

runners.name: Court Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Made a successful seasonal/handicap debut at Kempton (7f, Polytrack) last month and has run consistently well in three starts over 7f/1m since; hard to rule out, though not bred to appreciate this longer trip.
runners.comment: Has run consistently since handicapping, but isn't bred to appreciate this longer trip

runners.name: The Stone Power
runners.spotlight: Placed in his first three starts, over 7f/1m on the AW, but well held on handicap debut at Newbury (1m, good to soft; first start since being gelded) this month; may do better now upped in trip, though.
runners.comment: Well held on handicap debut at Newbury this month, but may do better now upped in trip

runners.name: Eljowhary
runners.spotlight: 140,000gns yearling; not without ability in three starts over 7f/1m on Tapeta/turf; should be fine up in trip for this handicap debut; worth considering.
runners.comment: Not without ability in three starts; should be fine up in trip for this handicap debut

runners.name: Aerial Silk
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in all three starts over 7f as a 2yo, but having been gelded was well held on his seasonal/handicap debut at Haydock (1m, good) last month; bred to appreciate the longer trip, even though he hasn't been shaping like it.
runners.comment: Well held on handicap debut; hasn't been shaping as though the longer trip will help

runners.name: Taradif
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in four starts over 6.5f-1m1f in France; makes his stable debut after four months off having been picked up for 13,000gns in April; likely he will need to improve.
runners.comment: Unplaced in four starts in France; makes his stable debut after four months off

races.race_name: Oldboys Fieldsman The Legend And Undercurfew Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Miss Nightcap
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in small-field maidens at Windsor and Newbury (both 6f, good to firm), the form of both races having been franked in the meantime; half-sister to a winner on the AW and should have no problem with the extra furlong; a player.
runners.comment: Runner-up in small-field 6f turf maidens; form of both races has been franked
runners.owner: E S-O Racing Club And Elaine Chivers

runners.name: Chasse Patate
runners.spotlight: Showed ability when third of nine on her Kempton debut (7f, Polytrack) last month (form worked out well) and ran to a similar level when second of seven at Lingfield (7f, good) 12 days later; should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Placed in both starts, over 7f on Polytrack and turf; should be thereabouts

runners.name: Vichenza
runners.spotlight: Third in both starts, on turf (6f, good), though her effort in a four-runner contest at Leicester was a step backwards from Salisbury debut; half-sister to an AW winner, but she does need to bounce back.
runners.comment: Third in both starts, on turf, but latest was a step backwards; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Hozam
runners.spotlight: Sent off 11-2 when third of nine on his debut here (8.6f) this month, not quite getting home after racing wide rounding the home turn; very much a player down in trip with the experience under his belt.
runners.comment: Third of nine on his debut here this month; very much a player down in trip

runners.name: Gallant Pioneer
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to winners City Cyclone (6f/7f AW; RPR 84), I Need Your Love (5.7f/6f including 2yo; 73), Cape Cyclone (1m; 57) and Sea The Sunset (2m2f hurdle); dam placed US 6.5f, half-sister to US 1m1f Grade 2 winner; already gelded; market should indicate expectations on debut.
runners.comment: Already gelded; market should indicate expectations on debut

runners.name: Elsa Tan
runners.spotlight: 3,500gns foal; sister to 6f-1m winner Silver Samurai (including 2yo; RPR 105), closely related to 5.3f/6f winner Unico (including AW 2yo; 73), half-sister to 7f-1m2f winner Boutan (including 2yo; 73); dam unraced half-sister to prolific 5f-7f winner My Sharona; something to like on breeding, but the outside stall could prove awkward for a newcomer.
runners.comment: Something to like on breeding, but the outside stall could prove awkward for a newcomer

runners.name: Rubistar
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in two 6f maidens on Tapeta/turf seven months apart (well backed on the first occasion); improvement required.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in two 6f maidens on Tapeta/turf seven months apart; improvement required

runners.name: Sonbelievable
runners.spotlight: 20-1 when a well-beaten seventh of 11 on his Windsor debut (1m, good to soft) this month; out of an AW winner but he needs to put that first effort behind him.
runners.comment: Well-beaten seventh of 11 on his Windsor debut this month; needs to put that behind him

runners.name: Cosmic Jive
runners.spotlight: Showed more than on his debut when seventh of 13 at Salisbury (7f, soft) this month; nicely bred, but handicaps may be more suitable after this.
runners.comment: Showed a bit more on his second start, but handicaps may be more suitable after this

runners.name: Rogue Federation
runners.spotlight: 25-1 when fifth of eight on his Yarmouth debut (6f, soft) this month; half-brother to three AW winners and should stay the extra furlong, but he does need to improve.
runners.comment: Fifth of eight on his Yarmouth debut this month; needs to improve

runners.name: Captive Beauty
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in two starts on turf in Ireland (6f/1m) seven months apart, though she did show a hint of ability on both occasions; changed hands for 4,000gns last month; out of an AW winner and worth a market check on stable debut.
runners.comment: Not without some ability in two starts on turf in Ireland; worth a market check

runners.name: Harry Rocket
runners.spotlight: 15-2 when last of six on his belated debut at Leicester (5f, good) last July; bred to handle the AW but this looks a tough task against the 3yos after such a long absence.
runners.comment: Last of six on his Leicester debut last July; this looks a tough task after a long absence

races.race_name: Francesi Wins Again For SF Racing Club Handicap

runners.name: Edwardtheninth
runners.spotlight: Made it 13th-time lucky at Newcastle (2m, Tapeta) 18 days ago, but that race was run at a farcically slow gallop; just 2lb higher, but not hard to have serious reservations.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle 18 days ago, but that race was run at a farcically slow gallop

runners.name: Qaaeadd
runners.spotlight: 0-11, his best effort when second of eight at Leicester (7f, good to firm) last July; not disgraced here (9.5f) in March, but has plenty to prove over this far.
runners.comment: 0-11; has run well a couple of times but has plenty to prove over this far

runners.name: The Craftymaster
runners.spotlight: Nine wins on the AW including two over C&D (course record reads 22111544); would have finished closer with a clear run when fifth of eight at Bath (2m1f, good) 15 days ago and very much of interest back here.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner and overall course record reads 22111544; very much of interest back here
runners.owner: R Miles, V Miles, T Stamp & Partner

runners.name: Four Fifty
runners.spotlight: Gradually went the wrong way for previous yard after making a successful debut and was comfortably held on stable debut at Bath (1m2f, good; first-time tongue-tie) this month; little to suggest this big step up in trip will make a difference.
runners.comment: Has gradually gone the wrong way since his successful debut; big step up in trip

runners.name: The Bay Warrior
runners.spotlight: Ran well when second of nine in returning cheekpieces at Leicester (1m4f, good) 15 days ago, but all seven wins have been on the AW; still 6lb lower than for the latest of those, but he attempts beyond 1m6f for the first time on his 63rd outing.
runners.comment: Seven AW wins, but he attempts beyond 1m6f for the first time on his 63rd outing

runners.name: Individualiste
runners.spotlight: Formerly a fair hurdler/chaser, but the wheels have come right off; no appeal despite another 6lb drop.
runners.comment: Formerly a fair hurdler/chaser, but the wheels have come right off; no appeal

runners.name: Ebendi
runners.spotlight: Just 1-30 and nothing for a long time to suggest he can double his tally.
runners.comment: Just 1-30 and nothing for a long time to suggest he can double his tally

runners.name: Man Of Riddles
runners.spotlight: Four wins on the AW include one over C&D in 2022, but nothing like as good these days; first-time blinkers need to have a dramatic effect.
runners.comment: C&D winner in 2022, but nothing like as good these days; blinkers on

races.race_name: Alfred Cove You Will Always Be A Star Handicap

runners.name: On Key
runners.spotlight: Record stands at 0-21 but he has been banging at the door on AW and turf since April; well drawn for one that races prominently and he should be in the thick of it once again.
runners.comment: Exposed 21-race maiden but banging at the door of late; in the mix once again

runners.name: Porfin
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner down the years; solid effort on turf three weeks ago and won't mind getting back on AW; looks the likeliest of Phil Mcentee's three runners.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly on turf three weeks ago; should give it another good go

runners.name: Sunlit Sea
runners.spotlight: Some fair efforts at a low level on AW over the winter; perhaps needed the run when down the field at Carlisle (6f, good) on recent return to action; another change of headgear today.
runners.comment: Chance on her 2yo best; needs new headgear to perk her up though

runners.name: Teardrops
runners.spotlight: Won three 6f handicaps at Newcastle over the winter when trained by Richard Spencer; two heavy defeats since joining this yard and risky, for all he's dropping in class this time.
runners.comment: Three Newcastle wins for Richard Spencer this winter; not shone in two runs for new yard

runners.name: Master Dandy
runners.spotlight: Began the year in hot form, winning 5f handicaps here and at Chelmsford; back to his last winning mark but he's barely beaten a rival on his last three runs.
runners.comment: Two 5f wins in January; struggled on his last three starts; needs a full revival

runners.name: Barry The Worm
runners.spotlight: Kept on well when scoring over C&D in November; ran okay back from a break in April and latest lacklustre run can be excused on account of soft ground; one to consider.
runners.comment: C&D winner in November; ground a possible excuse last time; revival on the cards

runners.name: Trust Sergei
runners.spotlight: Not a straightforward customer but he has plenty of ability for one operating at this level; back to form with a second of eight over 5f here eight weeks ago; no problem with the sixth furlong and he is more appealing than many.
runners.comment: Tricky customer but capable on his day and he ran well over 5f here last time; contender

runners.name: King David
runners.spotlight: Still seeking his first win for this stable and he has been well beaten on his last two starts; drawn wide on his return to sprinting; opposable.
runners.comment: Down in weights but has poor strike-rate & he's been well short of his best on last 2 runs

runners.name: Last Outlaw
runners.spotlight: Won two of his first four starts but his losing run is up to 15 and he was short of his best in three runs early in the year; sold for 5,000gns in February and new yard quick to get him back sprinting; worth a market check off his reduced mark.
runners.comment: On losing run but dropped in the weights and worth a market check on his stable debut

runners.name: White Umbrella
runners.spotlight: Four course wins, three of them over this trip, and she's well handicapped on form as recent as March; flopped on turf seven weeks ago but this should be more suitable.
runners.comment: Three C&D wins on her record; quiet on last three runs but on a dangerous mark now

runners.name: Alafdhal
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a hot spell in late 2025, winning three times (5f) either side of a close second over C&D; it has been tougher going this year and although Billy Loughnane's booking heightens interest, the draw could have been kinder.
runners.comment: Struggled so far this year and he has the outside stall to overcome today

runners.name: Em Jay Kay
runners.spotlight: Two minor 6f wins over the winter; not at his best on last two runs and now returns from 107 days off; drawn wide and others appeal more.
runners.comment: Two 6f AW wins this winter; not at best on last two runs and returning from 107 days off

runners.name: Thanks Dad
runners.spotlight: Popped up at Southwell (6f) in September when rated 1lb higher; little to shout about since and he is not a solid option; declared 8.35 Chepstow Saturday.
runners.comment: Southwell win last September came off 1lb higher; out of sorts since; runs 8.35 Saturday

races.race_name: Dapper Gee Gee Owners Handicap

runners.name: Level Up
runners.spotlight: The winner of 12 of his 92 starts, the latest success coming at Chelmsford in October off 1lb higher; several good runs to his name this time around and Billy Loughnane replaces a 7lb claimer today; one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Prolific winner, including C&D; Billy Loughnane replaces 7lb claimer today; contender

runners.name: Style King
runners.spotlight: Ran well for second over 6f here in April; two heavy defeats followed and although he ran a bit better at Lingfield last time, he was over 2l behind Shalaa Asker; now tried in another new form of headgear.
runners.comment: Second over 6f here in April; less good since, including behind Shalaa Asker latest

runners.name: Shalaa Asker
runners.spotlight: Four 5f wins since December, including C&D, with the latest coming at Lingfield 24 days ago; only 2lb higher and looks set for another prominent showing.
runners.comment: Four 5f wins since December, the latest 24 days ago; leading claims in this field

runners.name: The Fixer
runners.spotlight: Placed twice at Southwell (5f) in late 2025 but he has failed to fire on his last four starts; should be well treated but he is not one for maximum faith.
runners.comment: Two good Southwell runs in late 2025 but struggling this year; well treated but risky

runners.name: Blue Lancero
runners.spotlight: Some promise in two turf runs (7f) for Richard Hannon in May 2025; tailed off on stable debut (6f, Tapeta) nine months ago; drops in trip for handicap debut but far from solid.
runners.comment: Absent nine months and his stable debut was hardly full of promise

races.race_name: Thanks To TC Titch & The Team From SFRacing Club Handicap

runners.name: Pearly Squirrel
runners.spotlight: C&D win in March off 3lb lower and she ran a better race when third of 12 in a Class 5 over C&D 18 days later; up and down since but she can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Two good C&D runs in March and placed at Newbury last month; can't discount

runners.name: Skellig Isle
runners.spotlight: Stayed on from well back to win a 5f handicap at Chelmsford on her stable debut in March; beaten four times since and she is now going into new territory in terms of her stamina.
runners.comment: 5f Chelmsford win in March hasn't proved a springboard to better things; new trip today

runners.name: Lessay
runners.spotlight: C&D winner off 8lb higher in December; chased home Split Elevens here last month but not quite so good back here four weeks ago; apprentice takes off a useful 5lb; each-way shout.
runners.comment: C&D winner; chased home Split Elevens here in May; not quite so good latest

runners.name: Split Elevens
runners.spotlight: He was winning for the 13th time when seeing off eight rivals over C&D last month; backed it up with a close second at Lingfield 24 days ago; more appealing than many.
runners.comment: Five-time course winner who arrives in good form; more appealing than many

runners.name: Faster Bee
runners.spotlight: Flopped over C&D 51 days ago when reportedly unsuited by being unable to dominate; he had earlier won twice over C&D this year and he's not impossibly treated; goes without cheekpieces this time.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins this year; below par last time when unable to dominate; cheekpieces off today

runners.name: Clover Time
runners.spotlight: 15-race maiden; neither of his 7f runs have been convincing in terms of stamina and there are more persuasive options today.
runners.comment: 0-15 and neither 7f attempt has suggested it's the trip he wants

runners.name: Bomb Squad
runners.spotlight: Three wins over 7f this year, two of them here, and he turned in another solid effort at Southwell 38 days ago (6f); no obvious reason why he wouldn't go well once again.
runners.comment: Three 7f wins this year, two of them here; should be in the thick of it again

runners.name: Cooramook
runners.spotlight: 18-race maiden but there have been some near misses along the way; she was only sixth behind Split Elevens over C&D in May though and her losing run seems likely to be extended further.
runners.comment: Exposed maiden; conditions no problem but others are more persuasive for win purposes

runners.name: Harbour Vision
runners.spotlight: Course regular; his latest third (8.6f) was a remote one behind an easy winner but it was still a creditable effort; no sign of age denting his enthusiasm and he should make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Course regular who was a creditable 3rd over 8.6f 12 days ago; one to consider

races.race_name: Prix Eugene Adam (Group 2) (Turf)

runners.name: Pearled Majesty
runners.spotlight: Useful in three starts as a 2yo, which included a conditions race success at Chantilly, but done even better this campaign, completing a hat-trick in the Group 3 Prix Noailles (1m2.5f, good) in mid-April from the front; didn't run too badly in the Prix du Jockey Club 28 days ago and should have more to come, but Focus may challenge him if he tries to lead again; in the Arc.
runners.comment: Didn't run too badly in the Prix du Jockey Club and sound chance on the figures
runners.owner: Mme Christiane Head & Mauricio Delcher Sanchez

runners.name: Overnight
runners.spotlight: Closely related to plenty of smart performers; only made his debut in March, but has pushed on from that second-place finish, with two wins since, the most recent a 1m2f Listed event at Longchamp on good to soft, when stable/ownermate Focus was runner-up; nice scopey type with Group 1 entries, who shapes as though he has plenty more to come.
runners.comment: Won two since beaten on debut, including a 1m2f Listed event three weeks ago; sound chance

runners.name: Focus
runners.spotlight: Debut winner over 5f but didn't make any great progress until finishing third in a mile Listed event at Toulouse (good to soft) on his seasonal return; improved on that next time but finished runner-up to stable/ownermate Overnight having tried to make all in a 1m2f Listed contest on good to soft; Maxime Guyon prefers that colt again.
runners.comment: Finished 2nd to stable/ownermate Overnight last time; Maxime Guyon prefers that colt again

runners.name: Baroud
runners.spotlight: No wins as a 2yo but progressed as a 3yo, and won a 1m4f maiden here in April on good to soft before upping his level significantly when a close fifth of sixth in a 1m3f Group 3 at Longchamp; bit more needed on the figures, though, and has to prove he can handle a drop in distance.
runners.comment: Ran okay in a Group 3 last time, but the figures suggest he has a little bit to find
